  - [`amdsmi_vram_vendor_type_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} enum structure is removed.

  - [`amdsmi_vram_info_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} member named [`amdsmi_vram_vendor_type_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is changed to a character string.

  - [`amdsmi_get_gpu_vram_info`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} now no longer requires decoding the vendor name as an enum.

- Backwards compatibility for [`amdsmi_get_gpu_metrics_info()`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}'s,[`jpeg_activity`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}and [`vcn_activity`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} fields. Alternatively use [`xcp_stats.jpeg_busy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} or [`xcp_stats.vcn_busy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

  - Backwards compatibility is removed for [`jpeg_activity`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`vcn_activity`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} fields, if the [`jpeg_busy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} or [`vcn_busy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} field is available.

  - Providing both [`vcn_activity`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`jpeg_activity`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and XCP (partition) stats [`vcn_busy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`jpeg_busy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} caused confusion about which field to use. By removing backward compatibility, it is easier to identify the relevant field.

  - The [`jpeg_busy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} field increased in size (for supported ASICs), making backward compatibility unable to fully copy the structure into [`jpeg_activity`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

#### Optimized[\#](#id252 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Reduced [`amd-smi`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} CLI API calls needed to be called before reading or (re)setting GPU features. This improves overall runtime performance of the CLI.

- Removed partition information from the default [`amd-smi`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`static`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} CLI command.

  - Users can still retrieve the same data by calling [`amd-smi`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`amd-smi`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`static`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`-p`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, or [`amd-smi`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`partition`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`-c`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`-m`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`sudo`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`amd-smi`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`partition`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`-a`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

  - Reading [`current_compute_partition`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} may momentarily wake the GPU up. This is due to reading XCD registers, which is expected behavior. Changing partitions is not a trivial operation, [`current_compute_partition`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} SYSFS controls this action.

- Optimized CLI command [`amd-smi`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`topology`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in partition mode.

  - Reduced the number of [`amdsmi_topo_get_p2p_status`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} API calls to one fourth.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id253 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Removed duplicated GPU IDs when receiving events using the [`amd-smi`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`event`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} command.

- Fixed [`amd-smi`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`monitor`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} decoder utilization ([`DEC%`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}) not showing up on MI300 Series ASICs.

#### Known issues[\#](#id254 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`amd-smi`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`monitor`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} on Linux Guest systems triggers an attribute error.

### **Composable Kernel** (1.1.0)[\#](#id255 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id256 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for [`BF16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`F32`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, and [`F16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for 2D and 3D NGCHW grouped convolution backward data.

- Fully asynchronous HOST (CPU) arguments copy flow for CK grouped GEMM kernels.

- Support GKCYX for layout for grouped convolution forward (NGCHW/GKCYX/NGKHW, number of instances in instance factory for NGCHW/GKYXC/NGKHW has been reduced).

- Support for GKCYX layout for grouped convolution forward (NGCHW/GKCYX/NGKHW).

- Support for GKCYX layout for grouped convolution backward weight (NGCHW/GKCYX/NGKHW).

- Support for GKCYX layout for grouped convolution backward data (NGCHW/GKCYX/NGKHW).

- Support for Stream-K version of mixed [`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} / [`BF16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} GEMM.

- Support for Multiple D GEMM.

- GEMM pipeline for microscaling (MX) [`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} / [`FP6`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} / [`FP4`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} data types.

- Support for [`FP16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} 2:4 structured sparsity to universal GEMM.

- Support for Split K for grouped convolution backward data.

- Logit soft-capping support for fMHA forward kernels.

- Support for hdim as a multiple of 32 for FMHA (fwd/fwd_splitkv).

- Benchmarking support for tile engine GEMM.

- Ping-pong scheduler support for GEMM operation along the K dimension.

- Rotating buffer feature for CK_Tile GEMM.

- [`int8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} support for CK_TILE GEMM.

- Vectorize Transpose optimization for CK Tile.

- Asynchronous copy for gfx950.

#### Changed[\#](#id257 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Replaced the raw buffer load/store intrinsics with Clang20 built-ins.

- DL and DPP kernels are now enabled by default.

- Number of instances in instance factory for grouped convolution forward NGCHW/GKYXC/NGKHW has been reduced.

- Number of instances in instance factory for grouped convolution backward weight NGCHW/GKYXC/NGKHW has been reduced.

- Number of instances in instance factory for grouped convolution backward data NGCHW/GKYXC/NGKHW has been reduced.

#### Removed[\#](#id258 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Removed support for gfx940 and gfx941 targets.

#### Optimized[\#](#id259 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Optimized the GEMM multiply preshuffle and lds bypass with Pack of KGroup and better instruction layout.

### **HIP** 7.0.0[\#](#hip-7-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id260 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- New HIP APIs

  - [`hipLaunchKernelEx`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} dispatches the provided kernel with the given launch configuration and forwards the kernel arguments.

  - [`hipLaunchKernelExC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} launches a HIP kernel using a generic function pointer and the specified configuration.

  - [`hipDrvLaunchKernelEx`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} dispatches the device kernel represented by a HIP function object.

  - [`hipMemGetHandleForAddressRange`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} gets a handle for the address range requested.

  - [`__reduce_add_sync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`__reduce_min_sync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, and [`__reduce_max_sync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} functions added for aritimetic reduction across lanes of a warp, and [`__reduce_and_sync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`__reduce_or_sync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, and [`__reduce_xor_sync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} functions added for logical reduction. For details, see [Warp cross-lane functions](https://rocm.docs.amd.com/projects/HIP/en/latest/how-to/hip_cpp_language_extensions.html#warp-cross-lane-functions){.reference .external}.

- New support for Open Compute Project (OCP) floating-point [`FP4`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`FP6`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} as follows. For details, see [Low precision floating point document](https://rocm.docs.amd.com/projects/HIP/en/latest/reference/low_fp_types.html){.reference .external}.

  - Data types for [`FP4`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`FP6`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

  - HIP APIs for [`FP4`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`FP6`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, which are compatible with corresponding CUDA APIs.

  - HIP Extensions APIs for microscaling formats, which are supported on AMD GPUs.

- New [`wptr`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`rptr`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} values in [`ClPrint`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, for better logging in dispatch barrier methods.

- The [`_sync()`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} version of crosslane builtins such as [`shfl_sync()`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} are enabled by default. These can be disabled by setting the preprocessor macro [`HIP_DISABLE_WARP_SYNC_BUILTINS`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Added [`constexpr`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} operators for [`fp16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`bf16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Added warp level primitives: [`__syncwarp`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and reduce intrinsics (e.g. [`__reduce_add_sync()`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}).

- Support for the flags in APIs as following, now allows uncached memory allocation.

  - [`hipExtHostRegisterUncached`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, used in [`hipHostRegister`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

  - [`hipHostMallocUncached`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hipHostAllocUncached`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, used in [`hipHostMalloc`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hipHostAlloc`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- [`num_threads`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} total number of threads in the group. The legacy API size is alias.

- Added PCI CHIP ID information as the device attribute.

- Added new tests applications for OCP data types [`FP4`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`FP6`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- A new attribute in HIP runtime was implemented which exposes a new device capability of how many compute dies (chiplets, xcc) are available on a given GPU. Developers can get this attribute via the API [`hipDeviceGetAttribute`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, to make use of the best cache locality in a kernel, and optimize the Kernel launch grid layout, for performance improvement.

#### Changed[\#](#id261 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Some unsupported GPUs such as gfx9, gfx8 and gfx7 are deprecated on Microsoft Windows.

- Removal of beta warnings in HIP Graph APIs. All Beta warnings in usage of HIP Graph APIs are removed, they are now officially and fully supported.

- [`warpSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} has changed. In order to match the CUDA specification, the [`warpSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} variable is no longer [`constexpr`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. In general, this should be a transparent change; however, if an application was using [`warpSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} as a compile-time constant, it will have to be updated to handle the new definition. For more information, see the discussion of [`warpSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} within the [HIP C++ language extensions](https://rocm.docs.amd.com/projects/HIP/en/latest/how-to/hip_cpp_language_extensions.html#warpsize){.reference .external}.

- Behavior changes

  - [`hipGetLastError`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} now returns the error code which is the last actual error caught in the current thread during the application execution.

  - Cooperative groups in [`hipLaunchCooperativeKernelMultiDevice`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hipLaunchCooperativeKernel`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} functions, additional input parameter validation checks are added.

  - [`hipPointerGetAttributes`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} returns [`hipSuccess`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} instead of an error with invalid value [`hipErrorInvalidValue`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, in case [`NULL`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} host or attribute pointer is passed as input parameter. It now matches the functionality of [`cudaPointerGetAttributes`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} which changed with CUDA 11 and above releases.

  - [`hipFree`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} previously there was an implicit wait which was applicable for all memory allocations, for synchronization purpose. This wait is now disabled for allocations made with [`hipMallocAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hipMallocFromPoolAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, to match the behavior of CUDA API [`cudaFree`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

  - [`hipFreeAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} now returns [`hipSuccess`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} when the input pointer is NULL, instead of ` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`hipErrorInvalidValue`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} , to be consistent with [`hipFree`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

  - Exceptions occurring during a kernel execution will not abort the process anymore but will return an error unless core dump is enabled.

- Changes in hipRTC.

  - Removal of [`hipRTC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} symbols from HIP Runtime Library. Any application using [`hipRTC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} APIs should link explicitly with the [`hipRTC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} library. This makes the usage of [`hipRTC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} library on Linux the same as on Windows and matches the behavior of CUDA [`nvRTC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

  - [`hipRTC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} compilation The device code compilation now uses namespace [`__hip_internal`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, instead of the standard headers [`std`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, to avoid namespace collision.

  - Changes of datatypes from [`hipRTC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. Datatype definitions such as [`int64_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`uint64_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`int32_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, and [`uint32_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, etc. are removed to avoid any potential conflicts in some applications. HIP now uses internal datatypes instead, prefixed with [`__hip`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, for example, [`__hip_int64_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- HIP header clean up

  - Usage of STD headers, HIP header files only include necessary STL headers.

  - Deprecated structure [`HIP_MEMSET_NODE_PARAMS`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is removed. Developers can use the definition [`hipMemsetParams`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} instead.

- API signature/struct changes

  - API signatures are adjusted in some APIs to match corresponding CUDA APIs. Impacted APIs are as folloing:

    - [`hiprtcCreateProgram`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hiprtcCompileProgram`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpyHtoD`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipCtxGetApiVersion`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - HIP struct change in [`hipMemsetParams`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, it is updated and compatible with CUDA.

  - HIP vector constructor change in [`hipComplex`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} initialization now generates correct values. The affected constructors will be small vector types such as [`float2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`int4`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, etc.

- Stream Capture updates

  - Restricted stream capture mode, it is made in HIP APIs via adding the macro [`CHECK_STREAM_CAPTURE_SUPPORTED`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`()`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. In the previous HIP enumeration [`hipStreamCaptureMode`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, three capture modes were defined. With checking in the macro, the only supported stream capture mode is now [`hipStreamCaptureModeRelaxed`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. The rest are not supported, and the macro will return [`hipErrorStreamCaptureUnsupported`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. This update involves the following APIs, which is allowed only in relaxed stream capture mode:

    - [`hipMallocManaged`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemAdvise`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - Checks stream capture mode, the following APIs check the stream capture mode and return error codes to match the behavior of CUDA.

    - [`hipLaunchCooperativeKernelMultiDevice`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipEventQuery`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamAddCallback`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - Returns error during stream capture. The following HIP APIs now returns specific error [`hipErrorStreamCaptureUnsupported`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} on the AMD platform, but not always [`hipSuccess`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, to match behavior with CUDA:

    - [`hipDeviceSetMemPool`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemPoolCreate`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemPoolDestroy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipDeviceSetSharedMemConfig`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipDeviceSetCacheConfig`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpyWithStream`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

- Error code update Returned error/value codes are updated in the following HIP APIs to match the corresponding CUDA APIs.

  - Module Management Related APIs:

    - [`hipModuleLaunchKernel`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipExtModuleLaunchKernel`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipExtLaunchKernel`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipDrvLaunchKernelEx`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipLaunchKernel`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipLaunchKernelExC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipModuleLaunchCooperativeKernel`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipModuleLoad`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - Texture Management Related APIs: The following APIs update the return codes to match the behavior with CUDA:

    - [`hipTexObjectCreate`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, supports zero width and height for 2D image. If either is zero, will not return [`false`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

    - [`hipBindTexture2D`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, adds extra check, if pointer for texture reference or device is NULL, returns [`hipErrorNotFound`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

    - [`hipBindTextureToArray`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, if any NULL pointer is input for texture object, resource descriptor, or texture descriptor, returns error [`hipErrorInvalidChannelDescriptor`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, instead of [`hipErrorInvalidValue`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

    - [`hipGetTextureAlignmentOffset`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, adds a return code [`hipErrorInvalidTexture`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} when the texture reference pointer is NULL.

  - Cooperative Group Related APIs, more calidations are added in the following API implementation:

    - [`hipLaunchCooperativeKernelMultiDevice`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipLaunchCooperativeKernel`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

- Invalid stream input parameter handling In order to match the CUDA runtime behavior more closely, HIP APIs with streams passed as input parameters no longer check the stream validity. Previously, the HIP runtime returned an error code [`hipErrorContextIsDestroyed`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} if the stream was invalid. In CUDA version 12 and later, the equivalent behavior is to raise a segmentation fault. HIP runtime now matches the CUDA by causing a segmentation fault. The list of APIs impacted by this change are as follows:

  - Stream Management Related APIs

    - [`hipStreamGetCaptureInfo`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamGetPriority`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamGetFlags`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamDestroy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamAddCallback`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamQuery`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipLaunchHostFunc`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - Graph Management Related APIs

    - [`hipGraphUpload`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipGraphLaunch`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamBeginCaptureToGraph`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamBeginCapture`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamIsCapturing`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamGetCaptureInfo`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipGraphInstantiateWithParams`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - Memory Management Related APIs

    - [`hipMemcpyPeerAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpy2DValidateParams`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMallocFromPoolAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipFreeAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMallocAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpyAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpyToSymbolAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipStreamAttachMemAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemPrefetchAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipDrvMemcpy3D`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipDrvMemcpy3DAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipDrvMemcpy2DUnaligned`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpyParam2D`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpyParam2DAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpy2DArrayToArray`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpy2D`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpy2DAsync`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipDrvMemcpy2DUnaligned`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipMemcpy3D`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - Event Management Related APIs

    - [`hipEventRecord`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

    - [`hipEventRecordWithFlags`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

#### Optimized[\#](#id262 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

HIP runtime has the following functional improvements which improves runtime performance and user experience:

- Reduced usage of the lock scope in events and kernel handling.

  - Switches to [`shared_mutex`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for event validation, uses [`std::unique_lock`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in HIP runtime to create/destroy event, instead of [`scopedLock`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

  - Reduces the [`scopedLock`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in handling of kernel execution. HIP runtime now calls [`scopedLock`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} during kernel binary creation/initialization, doesn't call it again during kernel vector iteration before launch.

- Implementation of unifying managed buffer and kernel argument buffer so HIP runtime doesn't need to create/load a separate kernel argument buffer.

- Refactored memory validation, creates a unique function to validate a variety of memory copy operations.

- Improved kernel logging using demangling shader names.

- Advanced support for SPIRV, now kernel compilation caching is enabled by default. This feature is controlled by the environment variable [`AMD_COMGR_CACHE`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, for details, see [hip_rtc document](https://rocm.docs.amd.com/projects/HIP/en/latest/how-to/hip_rtc.html){.reference .external}.

- Programmatic support for scratch limits on the AMD Instinct MI300 and MI350 Series up GPU devices. More enumeration values were added in [`hipLimit_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} as following:

  - [`hipExtLimitScratchMin`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, minimum allowed value in bytes for scratch limit on the device.

  - [`hipExtLimitScratchMax`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, maximum allowed value in bytes for scratch limit on the device.

  - [`hipExtLimitScratchCurrent`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, current scratch limit threshold in bytes on the device. Must be between the value [`hipExtLimitScratchMin`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hipExtLimitScratchMax`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. Developers can now use the environment variable [`HSA_SCRATCH_SINGLE_LIMIT_ASYNC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to change the default allocation size with expected scratch limit in ROCR runtime. On top of it, this value can also be overwritten programmatically in the application using the HIP API [`hipDeviceSetLimit(hipExtLimitScratchCurrent,`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`value)`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to reset the scratch limit value.

- HIP runtime now enables peer-to-peer (P2P) memory copies to utilize all available SDMA engines, rather than being limited to a single engine. It also selects the best engine first to give optimal bandwidth.

- Improved launch latency for [`D2D`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} copies and [`memset`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} on MI300 Series.

- Introduced a threshold to handle the command submission patch to the GPU device(s), considering the synchronization with CPU, for performance improvement.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id263 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Error of "unable to find modules" in HIP clean up for code object module.

- The issue of incorrect return error [`hipErrorNoDevice`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, when a crash occurred on GPU device due to illegal operation or memory violation. HIP runtime now handles the failure on the GPU side properly and reports the precise error code based on the last error seen on the GPU.

- Failures in some framework test applications, HIP runtime fixed the bug in retrieving a memory object from the IPC memory handle.

- A crash in TensorFlow related application. HIP runtime now combines multiple definitions of [`callbackQueue`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} into a single function, in case of an exception, passes its handler to the application and provides corresponding error code.

- Fixed issue of handling the kernel parameters for the graph launch.

- Failures in roc-obj tools. HIP runtime now makes [`DEPRECATED`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} message in roc-obj tools as [`STDERR`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Support of [`hipDeviceMallocContiguous`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} flags in [`hipExtMallocWithFlags()`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. It now enables [`HSA_AMD_MEMORY_POOL_CONTIGUOUS_FLAG`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in the memory pool allocation on GPU device.

- Compilation failure, HIP runtime refactored the vector type alignment with [`__hip_vec_align_v`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- A numerical error/corruption found in Pytorch during graph replay. HIP runtime fixed the input sizes of kernel launch dimensions in hipExtModuleLaunchKernel for the execution of hipGraph capture.

- A crash during kernel execution in a customer application. The structure of kernel arguments was updated via adding the size of kernel arguments, and HIP runtime does validation before launch kernel with the structured arguments.

- Compilation error when using bfloat16 functions. HIP runtime removed the anonymous namespace from FP16 functions to resolve this issue.

#### Known issues[\#](#id264 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`hipLaunchHostFunc`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} returns an error during stream capture. Any application using [`hipLaunchHostFunc`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} might fail to capture graphs during stream capture, instead, it returns [`hipErrorStreamCaptureUnsupported`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Compilation failure in kernels via hiprtc when using option [`std=c++11`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

### **hipBLAS** (3.0.0)[\#](#hipblas-3-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id265 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Added the [`hipblasSetWorkspace()`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} API.

- Support for codecoverage tests.

#### Changed[\#](#id266 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- HIPBLAS_V2 API is the only available API using the [`hipComplex`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hipDatatype`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} types.

- Documentation updates.

- Verbose compilation for [`hipblas.cpp`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

#### Removed[\#](#id267 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`hipblasDatatype_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} type.

- [`hipComplex`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hipDoubleComplex`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} types.

- Support code for non-production gfx targets.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id268 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- The build time [`CMake`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} configuration for the dependency on [`hipBLAS-common`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is fixed.

- Compiler warnings for unhandled enumerations have been resolved.

### **hipBLASLt** (1.0.0)[\#](#hipblaslt-1-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id269 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Stream-K GEMM support has been enabled for the [`FP32`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`FP16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`BF16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, and [`BF8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} data types on the Instinct MI300A APU. To activate this feature, set the [`TENSILE_SOLUTION_SELECTION_METHOD`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} environment variable to [`2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, for example, [`export`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`TENSILE_SOLUTION_SELECTION_METHOD=2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Fused Swish/SiLU GEMM (enabled by [`HIPBLASLT_EPILOGUE_SWISH_EXT`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`HIPBLASLT_EPILOGUE_SWISH_BIAS_EXT`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}).

- Support for [`HIPBLASLT_EPILOGUE_GELU_AUX_BIAS`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for gfx942.

- [`HIPBLASLT_TUNING_USER_MAX_WORKSPACE`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to constrain the maximum workspace size for user offline tuning.

- [`HIPBLASLT_ORDER_COL16_4R16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`HIPBLASLT_ORDER_COL16_4R8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to [`hipblasLtOrder_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to support [`FP16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}/[`BF16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} swizzle GEMM and [`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} / [`BF8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} swizzle GEMM respectively.

- TF32 emulation on gfx950.

- Support for [`FP6`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`BF6`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, and [`FP4`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} on gfx950.

- Support for block scaling by setting [`HIPBLASLT_MATMUL_DESC_A_SCALE_MODE`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`HIPBLASLT_MATMUL_DESC_B_SCALE_MODE`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to [`HIPBLASLT_MATMUL_MATRIX_SCALE_VEC32_UE8M0`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

#### Changed[\#](#id270 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- The non-V2 APIs ([`GemmPreference`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmProblemType`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmEpilogue`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmTuning`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmInputs`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}) in the cpp header are now the same as the V2 APIs ([`GemmPreferenceV2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmProblemTypeV2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmEpilogueV2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmTuningV2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmInputsV2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}). The original non-V2 APIs are removed.

#### Removed[\#](#id271 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`HIPBLASLT_MATMUL_DESC_A_SCALE_POINTER_VEC_EXT`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`HIPBLASLT_MATMUL_DESC_B_SCALE_POINTER_VEC_EXT`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} are removed. Use the [`HIPBLASLT_MATMUL_DESC_A_SCALE_MODE`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`HIPBLASLT_MATMUL_DESC_B_SCALE_MODE`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} attributes to set scalar ([`HIPBLASLT_MATMUL_MATRIX_SCALE_SCALAR_32F`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}) or vector ([`HIPBLASLT_MATMUL_MATRIX_SCALE_OUTER_VEC_32F`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}) attributes.

- The [`hipblasltExtAMaxWithScale`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} API is removed.

#### Optimized[\#](#id272 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Improved performance for 8-bit ([`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} / [`BF8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} / [`I8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}) NN/NT cases by adding [`s_delay_alu`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to reduce stalls from dependent ALU operations on gfx12+.

- Improved performance for 8-bit and 16-bit ([`FP16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} / [`BF16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}) TN cases by enabling software dependency checks (Expert Scheduling Mode) under certain restrictions to reduce redundant hardware dependency checks on gfx12+.

- Improved performance for 8-bit, 16-bit, and 32-bit batched GEMM with a better heuristic search algorithm for gfx942.

#### Upcoming changes[\#](#id273 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- V2 APIs ([`GemmPreferenceV2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmProblemTypeV2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmEpilogueV2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmTuningV2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`GemmInputsV2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}) are deprecated.

### **hipCUB** (4.0.0)[\#](#hipcub-4-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id274 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- A new cmake option, [`BUILD_OFFLOAD_COMPRESS`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. When hipCUB is built with this option enabled, the [`--offload-compress`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} switch is passed to the compiler. This causes the compiler to compress the binary that it generates. Compression can be useful in cases where you are compiling for a large number of targets, since this often results in a large binary. Without compression, in some cases, the generated binary may become so large that symbols are placed out of range, resulting in linking errors. The new [`BUILD_OFFLOAD_COMPRESS`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} option is set to [`ON`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} by default.

- Single pass operators in [`agent/single_pass_scan_operators.hpp`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} which contains the following API:

  - [`BlockScanRunningPrefixOp`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - [`ScanTileStatus`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - [`ScanTileState`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - [`ReduceByKeyScanTileState`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

  - [`TilePrefixCallbackOp`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

- Support for gfx950.

- An overload of [`BlockScan::InclusiveScan`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} that accepts an initial value to seed the scan.

- An overload of [`WarpScan::InclusiveScan`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} that accepts an initial value to seed the scan.

- [`UnrolledThreadLoad`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`UnrolledCopy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, and [`ThreadLoadVolatilePointer`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} were added to align hipCUB with CUB.

- [`ThreadStoreVolatilePtr`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and the [`IterateThreadStore`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} struct were added to align hipCUB with CUB.

- [`hipcub::InclusiveScanInit`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for CUB parity.

#### Changed[\#](#id275 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- The CUDA backend now requires CUB, Thrust, and libcu++ 2.7.0. If they aren't found, they will be downloaded from the CUDA CCCL repository.

- Updated [`thread_load`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`thread_store`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to align hipCUB with CUB.

- All kernels now have hidden symbol visibility. All symbols now have inline namespaces that include the library version, (for example, [`hipcub::HIPCUB_300400_NS::symbol`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} instead of [`hipcub::symbol`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}), letting the user link multiple libraries built with different versions of hipCUB.

- Modified the broadcast kernel in warp scan benchmarks. The reported performance may be different to previous versions.

- The [`hipcub::detail::accumulator_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in rocPRIM backend has been changed to utilise [`rocprim::accumulator_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- The usage of [`rocprim::invoke_result_binary_op_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} has been replaced with [`rocprim::accumulator_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

#### Removed[\#](#id276 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- The AMD GPU targets [`gfx803`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`gfx900`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} are no longer built by default. If you want to build for these architectures, specify them explicitly in the [`AMDGPU_TARGETS`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} cmake option.

- Deprecated [`hipcub::AsmThreadLoad`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is removed, use [`hipcub::ThreadLoad`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} instead.

- Deprecated [`hipcub::AsmThreadStore`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is removed, use [`hipcub::ThreadStore`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} instead.

- Deprecated [`BlockAdjacentDifference::FlagHeads`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`BlockAdjacentDifference::FlagTails`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`BlockAdjacentDifference::FlagHeadsAndTails`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} have been removed.

- This release removes support for custom builds on gfx940 and gfx941.

- Removed C++14 support. Only C++17 is supported.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id277 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Fixed an issue where [`Sort(keys,`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`compare_op,`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`valid_items,`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`oob_default)`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in [`block_merge_sort.hpp`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} would not fill in elements that are out of range (items after [`valid_items`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}) with [`oob_default`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Fixed an issue where [`ScatterToStripedFlagged`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in [`block_exhange.hpp`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} was calling the wrong function.

#### Known issues[\#](#id278 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`BlockAdjacentDifference::FlagHeads`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`BlockAdjacentDifference::FlagTails`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`BlockAdjacentDifference::FlagHeadsAndTails`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} have been removed from hipCUB's CUB backend. They were already deprecated as of version 2.12.0 of hipCUB and they were removed from CCCL (CUB) as of CCCL's 2.6.0 release.

- [`BlockScan::InclusiveScan`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for the CUDA backend does not compute the block aggregate correctly when passing an initial value parameter. This behavior is not matched by the AMD backend.

#### Upcoming changes[\#](#id279 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`BlockAdjacentDifference::FlagHeads`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`BlockAdjacentDifference::FlagTails`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`BlockAdjacentDifference::FlagHeadsAndTails`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} were deprecated as of version 2.12.0 of hipCUB, and will be removed from the rocPRIM backend in a future release for the next ROCm major version (ROCm 7.0.0).

### **hipFFT** (1.0.20)[\#](#hipfft-1-0-20 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id280 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for gfx950.

#### Removed[\#](#id281 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Removed hipfft-rider legacy compatibility from clients.

- Removed support for the gfx940 and gfx941 targets from the client programs.

- Removed backward compatibility symlink for include directories.

### **hipfort** (0.7.0)[\#](#hipfort-0-7-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id282 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Documentation clarifying how hipfort is built for the CUDA platform.

#### Changed[\#](#id283 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Updated and reorganized documentation for clarity and consistency.

### **HIPIFY** (20.0.0)[\#](#hipify-20-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id284 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- CUDA 12.9.1 support.

- cuDNN 9.11.0 support.

- cuTENSOR 2.2.0.0 support.

- LLVM 20.1.8 support.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id285 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`hipDNN`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} support is removed by default.

- [#1859](https://github.com/ROCm/HIPIFY/issues/1859){.reference .external}\[hipify-perl\] Fix warnings on unsupported Driver or Runtime APIs which were erroneously not reported.

- [#1930](https://github.com/ROCm/HIPIFY/issues/1930){.reference .external} Revise [`JIT`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`API`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- [#1962](https://github.com/ROCm/HIPIFY/issues/1962){.reference .external} Support for cuda-samples helper headers.

- [#2035](https://github.com/ROCm/HIPIFY/issues/2035){.reference .external} Removed [`const_cast<const`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`char**>;`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in [`hiprtcCreateProgram`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hiprtcCompileProgram`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

### **hipRAND** (3.0.0)[\#](#hiprand-3-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id286 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for gfx950.

#### Changed[\#](#id287 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Deprecated the hipRAND Fortran API in favor of hipfort.

#### Removed[\#](#id288 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Removed C++14 support, so only C++17 is supported.

### **hipSOLVER** (3.0.0)[\#](#hipsolver-3-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id289 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Added compatibility-only functions:

  - csrlsvqr

    - [`hipsolverSpCcsrlsvqr`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverSpZcsrlsvqr`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id290 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Corrected the value of [`lwork`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} returned by various [`bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} functions to be consistent with CUDA cuSOLVER. The following functions now return [`lwork`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} so that the workspace size (in bytes) is [`sizeof(T)`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`*`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`lwork`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, rather than [`lwork`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. To restore the original behavior, set the environment variable [`HIPSOLVER_BUFFERSIZE_RETURN_BYTES`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

  - [`hipsolverXorgbr_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXorgqr_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXorgtr_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXormqr_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXormtr_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXgesvd_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXgesvdj_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXgesvdBatched_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXgesvdaStridedBatched_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXsyevd_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXsyevdx_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXsyevj_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXsyevjBatched_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXsygvd_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXsygvdx_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXsygvj_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXsytrd_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`hipsolverXsytrf_bufferSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

### **hipSPARSE** (4.0.1)[\#](#hipsparse-4-0-1 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id291 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`int8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`int32`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, and [`float16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} data types to [`hipDataTypeToHCCDataType`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} so that sparse matrix descriptors can be used with them.

- Half float mixed precision to [`hipsparseAxpby`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} where X and Y use [`float16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and the result and compute type use [`float`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Half float mixed precision to [`hipsparseSpVV`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} where X and Y use [`float16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and the result and compute type use [`float`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Half float mixed precision to [`hipsparseSpMM`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} where A and B use [`float16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and C and the compute type use [`float`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Half float mixed precision to [`hipsparseSDDMM`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} where A and B use [`float16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and C and the compute type use [`float`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Half float uniform precision to the [`hipsparseScatter`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hipsparseGather`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} routines.

- Half float uniform precision to the [`hipsparseSDDMM`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} routine.

- [`int8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} precision to the [`hipsparseCsr2cscEx2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} routine.

- The [`almalinux`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} operating system name to correct the GFortran dependency.

#### Changed[\#](#id292 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Switched to defaulting to C++17 when building hipSPARSE from source. Previously hipSPARSE was using C++14 by default.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id293 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Fixed a compilation [issue](https://github.com/ROCm/hipSPARSE/issues/555){.reference .external} related to using [`std::filesystem`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and C++14.

- Fixed an issue where the clients-common package was empty by moving the [`hipsparse_clientmatrices.cmake`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hipsparse_mtx2csr`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} files to it.

#### Known issues[\#](#id294 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- In [`hipsparseSpSM_solve()`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, the external buffer is passed as a parameter. This does not match the CUDA cuSPARSE API. This extra external buffer parameter will be removed in a future release. For now, this extra parameter can be ignored and nullptr passed in because it is unused internally.

### **hipSPARSELt** (0.2.4)[\#](#hipsparselt-0-2-4 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id295 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for the LLVM target gfx950.

- Support for the following data type combinations for the LLVM target gfx950:

  - [`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}(E4M3) inputs, [`F32`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} output, and [`F32`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} Matrix Core accumulation.

  - [`BF8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}(E5M2) inputs, [`F32`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} output, and [`F32`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} Matrix Core accumulation.

- Support for ROC-TX if [`HIPSPARSELT_ENABLE_MARKER=1`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is set.

- Support for the cuSPARSELt v0.6.3 backend.

#### Removed[\#](#id296 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for LLVM targets gfx940 and gfx941 has been removed.

- [`hipsparseLtDatatype_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} has been removed.

#### Optimized[\#](#id297 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Improved the library loading time.

- Provided more kernels for the [`FP16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} data type.

### **hipTensor** (2.0.0)[\#](#hiptensor-2-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id298 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Element-wise binary operation support.

- Element-wise trinary operation support.

- Support for GPU target gfx950.

- Dynamic unary and binary operator support for element-wise operations and permutation.

- CMake check for [`f8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} datatype availability.

- [`hiptensorDestroyOperationDescriptor`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to free all resources related to the provided descriptor.

- [`hiptensorOperationDescriptorSetAttribute`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to set attribute of a [`hiptensorOperationDescriptor_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} object.

- [`hiptensorOperationDescriptorGetAttribute`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to retrieve an attribute of the provided [`hiptensorOperationDescriptor_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} object.

- [`hiptensorCreatePlanPreference`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to allocate the [`hiptensorPlanPreference_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and enabled users to limit the applicable kernels for a given plan or operation.

- [`hiptensorDestroyPlanPreference`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to free all resources related to the provided preference.

- [`hiptensorPlanPreferenceSetAttribute`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to set attribute of a [`hiptensorPlanPreference_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} object.

- [`hiptensorPlanGetAttribute`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to retrieve information about an already-created plan.

- [`hiptensorEstimateWorkspaceSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to determine the required workspace size for the given operation.

- [`hiptensorCreatePlan`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to allocate a [`hiptensorPlan_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} object, select an appropriate kernel for a given operation and prepare a plan that encodes the execution.

- [`hiptensorDestroyPlan`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to free all resources related to the provided plan.

#### Changed[\#](#id299 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Removed architecture support for gfx940 and gfx941.

- Generalized opaque buffer for any descriptor.

- Replaced [`hipDataType`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorDataType_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for all supported types, for example, [`HIP_R_32F`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to [`HIPTENSOR_R_32F`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorComputeType_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorComputeDescriptor_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for all supported types.

- Replaced [`hiptensorInitTensorDescriptor`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorCreateTensorDescriptor`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Changed handle type and API usage from [`*handle`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to [`handle`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorContractionDescriptor_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hipTensorOperationDescriptor_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorInitContractionDescriptor`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorCreateContraction`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorContractionFind_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorPlanPreference_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorInitContractionFind`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorCreatePlanPreference`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorContractionGetWorkspaceSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorEstimateWorkspaceSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`HIPTENSOR_WORKSPACE_RECOMMENDED`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`HIPTENSOR_WORKSPACE_DEFAULT`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorContractionPlan_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorPlan_t`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorInitContractionPlan`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorCreatePlan`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorContraction`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorContract`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorPermutation`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorPermute`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorReduction`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorReduce`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorElementwiseBinary`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorElementwiseBinaryExecute`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Replaced [`hiptensorElementwiseTrinary`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`hiptensorElementwiseTrinaryExecute`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Removed function [`hiptensorReductionGetWorkspaceSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

### **llvm-project** (20.0.0)[\#](#llvm-project-20-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id300 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- The compiler [`-gsplit-dwarf`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} option to enable the generation of separate debug information file at compile time. When used, separate debug information files are generated for host and for each offload architecture. For additional information, see [DebugFission](https://gcc.gnu.org/wiki/DebugFission){.reference .external}.

- [`llvm-flang`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, AMD's next-generation Fortran compiler. It's a re-implementation of the Fortran frontend that can be found at [`llvm/llvm-project/flang`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} on GitHub.

- Comgr support for an in-memory virtual file system (VFS) for storing temporary files generated during intermediate compilation steps to improve performance in the device library link step.

- Compiler support of a new target-specific builtin [`__builtin_amdgcn_processor_is`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for late or deferred queries of the current target processor, and [`__builtin_amdgcn_is_invocable`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to determine the current target processor ability to invoke a particular builtin.

- HIPIFY support for CUDA 12.9.1 APIs. Added support for all new device and host APIs, including FP4, FP6, and FP128, and support for the corresponding ROCm HIP equivalents.

#### Changed[\#](#id301 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Updated clang/llvm to AMD clang version 20.0.0 (equivalent to LLVM 20.0.0 with additional out-of-tree patches).

- HIPCC Perl scripts ([`hipcc.pl`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`hipconfig.pl`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}) have been removed from this release.

#### Optimized[\#](#id302 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Improved compiler memory load and store instructions.

#### Upcoming changes[\#](#id303 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`__AMDGCN_WAVEFRONT_SIZE__`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} macro and HIP's [`warpSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} variable as [`constexpr`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} are deprecated and will be disabled in a future release. Users are encouraged to update their code if needed to ensure future compatibility. For more information, see [AMDGCN_WAVEFRONT_SIZE deprecation](https://rocm.docs.amd.com/en/docs-7.0.0/about/release-notes.html#amdgpu-wavefront-size-compiler-macro-deprecation){.reference .external}.

- The [`roc-obj-ls`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`roc-obj-extract`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} tools are deprecated. To extract all Clang offload bundles into separate code objects use [`llvm-objdump`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`--offloading`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`<file>`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. For more information, see [Changes to ROCm Object Tooling](https://rocm.docs.amd.com/en/docs-7.0.0/about/release-notes.html#changes-to-rocm-object-tooling){.reference .external}.

### **MIGraphX** (2.13.0)[\#](#migraphx-2-13-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id304 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for OCP [`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} on AMD Instinct MI350X GPUs.

- Support for PyTorch 2.7 via Torch-MIGraphX.

- Support for the Microsoft ONNX Contrib Operators (Self) Attention, RotaryEmbedding, QuickGelu, BiasAdd, BiasSplitGelu, SkipLayerNorm.

- Support for Sigmoid and AddN TensorFlow operators.

- GroupQuery Attention support for LLMs.

- Support for edge mode in the ONNX Pad operator.

- ONNX runtime Python driver.

- FLUX e2e example.

- C++ and Python APIs to save arguments to a graph as a msgpack file, and then read the file back.

- rocMLIR fusion for kv-cache attention.

- Introduced a check for file-write errors.

#### Changed[\#](#id305 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`quantize_bf16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for quantizing the model to [`BF16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} has been made visible in the MIGraphX user API.

- Print additional kernel/module information in the event of compile failure.

- Use hipBLASLt instead of rocBLAS on newer GPUs.

- 1x1 convolutions are now rewritten to GEMMs.

- [`BF16::max`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is now represented by its encoding rather than its expected value.

- Direct warnings now go to [`cout`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} rather [`cerr`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- [`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} uses hipBLASLt rather than rocBLAS.

- ONNX models are now topologically sorted when nodes are unordered.

- Improved layout of Graphviz output.

- Enhanced debugging for migraphx-driver: consumed environment variables are printed, timestamps and duration are added to the summary.

- Add a trim size flag to the verify option for migraphx-driver.

- Node names are printed to track parsing within the ONNX graph when using the [`MIGRAPHX_TRACE_ONNX_PARSER`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} flag.

- Update accuracy checker to output test data with the [`--show-test-data`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} flag.

- The [`MIGRAPHX_TRACE_BENCHMARKING`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} option now allows the problem cache file to be updated after finding the best solution.

#### Removed[\#](#id306 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`ROCM_USE_FLOAT8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} macro.

- The [`BF16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} GEMM test was removed for Navi21, as it is unsupported by rocBLAS and hipBLASLt on that platform.

#### Optimized[\#](#id307 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Use common average in [`compile_ops`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to reduce run-to-run variations when tuning.

- Improved the performance of the TopK operator.

- Conform to a single layout (NHWC or NCHW) during compilation rather than combining two.

- Slice Channels Conv Optimization (slice output fusion).

- Horizontal fusion optimization after pointwise operations.

- Reduced the number of literals used in [`GridSample`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} linear sampler.

- Fuse multiple outputs for pointwise operations.

- Fuse reshapes on pointwise inputs for MLIR output fusion.

- MUL operation not folded into the GEMM when the GEMM is used more than once.

- Broadcast not fused after convolution or GEMM MLIR kernels.

- Avoid reduction fusion when operator data-types mismatch.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id308 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Compilation workaround ICE in clang 20 when using [`views::transform`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Fix bug with [`reshape_lazy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in MLIR.

- Quantizelinear fixed for Nearbyint operation.

- Check for empty strings in ONNX node inputs for operations like Resize.

- Parse Resize fix: only check [`keep_aspect_ratio_policy`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} attribute for sizes input.

- Nonmaxsuppression: fixed issue where identical boxes/scores not ordered correctly.

- Fixed a bug where events were created on the wrong device in a multi-gpu scenario.

- Fixed out of order keys in value for comparisons and hashes when caching best kernels.

- Fixed Controlnet MUL types do not match error.

- Fixed check for scales if ROI input is present in Resize operation.

- Einsum: Fixed a crash on empty squeeze operations.

### **MIOpen** (3.5.0)[\#](#miopen-3-5-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id309 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- \[Conv\] Misa kernels for gfx950.

- \[Conv\] Enabled Split-K support for CK backward data solvers (2D).

- \[Conv\] Enabled CK wrw solver on gfx950 for the [`BF16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} data type.

- \[BatchNorm\] Enabled NHWC in OpenCL.

- Grouped convolution + activation fusion.

- Grouped convolution + bias + activation fusion.

- Composable Kernel (CK) can now be built inline as part of MIOpen.

#### Changed[\#](#id310 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Changed to using the median value with outliers removed when deciding on the best solution to run.

- \[Conv\] Updated the igemm asm solver.

#### Optimized[\#](#id311 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- \[BatchNorm\] Optimized NHWC OpenCL kernels and improved heuristics.

- \[RNN\] Dynamic algorithm optimization.

- \[Conv\] Eliminated redundant clearing of output buffers.

- \[RNN\] Updated selection heuristics.

- Updated tuning for the AMD Instinct MI300 Series.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id312 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Fixed a segmentation fault when the user specified a smaller workspace than what was required.

- Fixed a layout calculation logic error that returned incorrect results and enabled less restrictive layout selection.

- Fixed memory access faults in misa kernels due to out-of-bounds memory usage.

- Fixed a performance drop on the gfx950 due to transpose kernel use.

- Fixed a memory access fault caused by not allocating enough workspace.

- Fixed a name typo that caused kernel mismatches and long startup times.

### **MIVisionX** (3.3.0)[\#](#mivisionx-3-3-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id313 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support to enable/disable BatchPD code in VX_RPP extensions by checking the RPP_LEGACY_SUPPORT flag.

#### Changed[\#](#id314 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- VX_RPP extension: Version 3.1.0 release.

- Update the parameters and kernel API of Blur, Fog, Jitter, LensCorrection, Rain, Pixelate, Vignette and ResizeCrop wrt tensor kernels replacing the legacy BatchPD API calls in VX_RPP extensions.

#### Known issues[\#](#id315 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Installation on RHEL and SLES requires the manual installation of the [`FFMPEG`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`OpenCV`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} dev packages.

#### Upcoming changes[\#](#id316 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Optimized audio augmentations support for VX_RPP.

### **RCCL** (2.26.6)[\#](#id317 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id318 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for the extended fine-grained system memory pool.

- Support for gfx950.

- Support for [`unroll=1`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in device-code generation to improve performance.

- Set a default of 112 channels for a single node with [`8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`*`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`gfx950`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Enabled LL128 protocol on the gfx950.

- The ability to choose the unroll factor at runtime using [`RCCL_UNROLL_FACTOR`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. This can be set at runtime to 1, 2, or 4. This change currently increases compilation and linking time because it triples the number of kernels generated.

- Added MSCCL support for AllGather multinode on the gfx942 and gfx950 (for instance, 16 and 32 GPUs). To enable this feature, set the environment variable [`RCCL_MSCCL_FORCE_ENABLE=1`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. The maximum message size for MSCCL AllGather usage is [`12292`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`*`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`sizeof(datatype)`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`*`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`nGPUs`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Thread thresholds for LL/LL128 are selected in Tuning Models for the AMD Instinct MI300X. This impacts the number of channels used for AllGather and ReduceScatter. The channel tuning model is bypassed if [`NCCL_THREAD_THRESHOLDS`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`NCCL_MIN_NCHANNELS`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, or [`NCCL_MAX_NCHANNELS`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} are set.

- Multi-node tuning for AllGather, AllReduce, and ReduceScatter that leverages LL/LL64/LL128 protocols to use nontemporal vector load/store for tunable message size ranges.

- LL/LL128 usage ranges for AllReduce, AllGather, and ReduceScatter are part of the tuning models, which enable architecture-specific tuning in conjunction with the existing Rome Models scheme in RCCL.

- Two new APIs are exposed as part of an initiative to separate RCCL code. These APIs are [`rcclGetAlgoInfo`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`rcclFuncMaxSendRecvCount`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. However, user-level invocation requires that RCCL be built with [`RCCL_EXPOSE_STATIC`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} enabled.

#### Changed[\#](#id319 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Compatibility with NCCL 2.23.4.

- Compatibility with NCCL 2.24.3.

- Compatibility with NCCL 2.25.1.

- Compatibility with NCCL 2.26.6.

#### Optimized[\#](#id320 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Improved the performance of the [`FP8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} Sum operation by upcasting to [`FP16`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id321 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Resolved an issue when using more than 64 channels when multiple collectives are used in the same [`ncclGroup()`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} call.

- Fixed unit test failures in tests ending with the [`ManagedMem`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`ManagedMemGraph`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} suffixes.

- Fixed a suboptimal algorithmic switching point for AllReduce on the AMD Instinct MI300X.

- Fixed broken functionality within the LL protocol on gfx950 by disabling inlining of LLGenericOp kernels.

- Fixed the known issue "When splitting a communicator using [`ncclCommSplit`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} in some GPU configurations, MSCCL initialization can cause a segmentation fault" with a design change to use [`comm`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} instead of [`rank`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for [`mscclStatus`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}. The global map for [`comm`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to [`mscclStatus`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is still not thread safe but should be explicitly handled by mutexes for read-write operations. This is tested for correctness, but there is a plan to use a thread-safe map data structure in an upcoming release.

### **rocAL** (2.3.0)[\#](#rocal-2-3-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id322 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Extended support to rocAL's video decoder to use rocDecode hardware decoder.

- Setup - installs rocdecode dev packages for Ubuntu, RedHat, and SLES.

- Setup - installs turbojpeg dev package for Ubuntu and Redhat.

- rocAL's image decoder has been extended to support the rocJPEG hardware decoder.

- Numpy reader support for reading npy files in rocAL.

- Test case for numpy reader in C++ and python tests.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id323 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`TurboJPEG`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} no longer needs to be installed manually. It is now installed by the package installer.

- Hardware decode no longer requires that ROCm be installed with the [`graphics`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} usecase.

#### Known issues[\#](#id324 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Package installation on SLES requires manually installing [`TurboJPEG`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Package installation on RHEL and SLES requires manually installing the [`FFMPEG`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`Dev`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} package.

#### Upcoming changes[\#](#id325 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- rocJPEG support for JPEG decode.

### **rocALUTION** (4.0.0)[\#](#rocalution-4-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id326 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for gfx950.

#### Changed[\#](#id327 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Switch to defaulting to C++17 when building rocALUTION from source. Previously rocALUTION was using C++14 by default.

#### Optimized[\#](#id328 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Improved the user documentation.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id329 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Fix for GPU hashing algorithm when not compiling with -O2/O3.

### **rocBLAS** (5.0.0)[\#](#rocblas-5-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id330 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for gfx950.

- Internal API logging for [`gemm`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} debugging using [`ROCBLAS_LAYER`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`=`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`8`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Support for the AOCL 5.0 gcc build as a client reference library.

- The use of [`PkgConfig`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for client reference library fallback detection.

#### Changed[\#](#id331 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is now passed on during compilation for a Tensile build.

- The default atomics mode is changed from [`allowed`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} to [`not`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`allowed`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

#### Removed[\#](#id332 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support code for non-production gfx targets.

- [`rocblas_hgemm_kernel_name`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`rocblas_sgemm_kernel_name`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, and [`rocblas_dgemm_kernel_name`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} API functions.

- The use of [`warpSize`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} as a constexpr.

- The use of deprecated behavior of [`hipPeekLastError`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- [`rocblas_float8.h`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`rocblas_hip_f8_impl.h`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} files.

- [`rocblas_gemm_ex3`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, [`rocblas_gemm_batched_ex3`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}, and [`rocblas_gemm_strided_batched_ex3`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} API functions.

#### Optimized[\#](#id333 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Optimized [`gemm`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} by using [`gemv`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} kernels when applicable.

- Optimized [`gemv`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for small [`m`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`n`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with a large batch count on gfx942.

- Improved the performance of Level 1 [`dot`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for all precisions and variants when [`N`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`>`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`100000000`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} on gfx942.

- Improved the performance of Level 1 [`asum`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`nrm2`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for all precisions and variants on gfx942.

- Improved the performance of Level 2 [`sger`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} (single precision) on gfx942.

- Improved the performance of Level 3 [`dgmm`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} for all precisions and variants on gfx942.

#### Resolved issues[\#](#id334 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Fixed environment variable path-based logging to append multiple handle outputs to the same file.

- Support numerics when [`trsm`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is running with [`rocblas_status_perf_degraded`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

- Fixed the build dependency installation of [`joblib`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} on some operating systems.

- Return [`rocblas_status_internal_error`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} when [`rocblas_[set,get]_`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}` `{.docutils .literal .notranslate}[`[matrix,vector]`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is called with a host pointer in place of a device pointer.

- Reduced the default verbosity level for internal GEMM backend information.

- Updated from the deprecated rocm-cmake to ROCmCMakeBuildTools.

- Corrected AlmaLinux GFortran package dependencies.

#### Upcoming changes[\#](#id335 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Deprecated the use of negative indices to indicate the default solution is being used for [`gemm_ex`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} with [`rocblas_gemm_algo_solution_index`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre}.

### **ROCdbgapi** (0.77.3)[\#](#rocdbgapi-0-77-3 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id336 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for the [`gfx950`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} architectures.

#### Removed[\#](#id337 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- Support for the [`gfx940`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} and [`gfx941`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} architectures.

### **rocDecode** (1.0.0)[\#](#rocdecode-1-0-0 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

#### Added[\#](#id338 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- VP9 IVF container file parsing support in bitstream reader.

- CTest for VP9 decode on bitstream reader.

- HEVC/AVC/AV1/VP9 stream syntax error handling.

- HEVC stream bit depth change handling and DPB buffer size change handling through decoder reconfiguration.

- AVC stream DPB buffer size change handling through decoder reconfiguration.

- A new avcodec-based decoder built as a separate [`rocdecode-host`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} library.

#### Changed[\#](#id339 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- rocDecode now uses the Cmake [`CMAKE_PREFIX_PATH`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} directive.

- Changed asserts in query API calls in RocVideoDecoder utility class to error reports, to avoid hard stop during query in case error occurs and to let the caller decide actions.

- [`libdrm_amdgpu`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} is now explicitly linked with rocdecode.

#### Removed[\#](#id340 "Link to this heading"){.headerlink}

- [`GetStream()`{.docutils .literal .notranslate}]{.pre} interface call from RocVideoDecoder utility class.
